title: Pipelines
text: A xPipeline is a concatenation of functions operating on xData objects.

title: Pipelines
text:
Function signature
Every pipeline function takes a single xData argument (plus any keyword arguments) and returns the (modified) container.

def add_data_entity(pdata, name="myname", size=1):
    """Add a simple data entity to the container.

    A valid pipeline function has exactly one positional argument, the xData
    object being processed, and may have any number of keyword arguments
    (which are logged alongside the pipeline step).
    """
    pdata.add([1, 2, 3] * size, name=name)
    # every pipeline function must return the pdata object, making explicit
    # that it modifies and passes on the container
    return pdata

title: Pipelines
text:
Initialization

from xileh import xData, xPipeline

pl = xPipeline("test_pipeline", silent=True)
pl.add_steps(
    ("add_to_data", add_data_entity),
    ("add_to_data_2", add_data_entity, {"name": "another_test"}),
)
pl

<xileh.core.pipeline.xPipeline object at 0x1041720d0>
Pipeline name: test_pipeline
Steps: 
    -> 'add_to_data'
    -> 'add_to_data_2'


The internal step list holds tuples of (name, function, kwargs):

pl.steps

[('add_to_data',
  <function __main__.add_data_entity(pdata, name='myname', size=1)>,
  {}),
 ('add_to_data_2',
  <function __main__.add_data_entity(pdata, name='myname', size=1)>,
  {'name': 'another_test'})]


A single step can be looked up by name:

pl.get_step("add_to_data_2")

(('add_to_data_2',
  <function __main__.add_data_entity(pdata, name='myname', size=1)>,
  {'name': 'another_test'}),
 1)

title: Pipelines
text:
Evaluating

pdata = xData([], name="outercontainer")
pl.eval(pdata)
pdata.get_container_names()

['outercontainer', 'myname', 'another_test']

title: Pipelines
text:
Modifying steps

def foo(pdata, k="abc"):
    return pdata


# replace a step entirely
pl.replace_step("add_to_data", ("new_step", foo))

# update the kwargs of a step
pl.add_steps(("test_step", foo))
pl.set_step_kwargs("test_step", k="cde")

# remove steps
pl.remove_step("new_step")
pl.remove_steps(["add_to_data_2"])
pl

<xileh.core.pipeline.xPipeline object at 0x1041720d0>
Pipeline name: test_pipeline
Steps: 
    -> 'test_step'

title: Pipelines
text:
Composing pipelines
Because steps are plain tuples, pipelines compose by splatting another pipeline’s steps:

def print_foo(pdata):
    for name in pdata.get_container_names():
        print(f"{name} - {pdata[name].data}")
    return pdata


combined = xPipeline("combined", silent=True)
combined.add_steps(
    ("print_before", print_foo),
    *pl.steps,
    ("print_after", print_foo),
)
combined

<xileh.core.pipeline.xPipeline object at 0x104190750>
Pipeline name: combined
Steps: 
    -> 'print_before'
    -> 'test_step'
    -> 'print_after'

title: Pipelines
text:
Early stopping
A pipeline function can short-circuit the remaining steps by setting early_stop in the container header (e.g. when loading data finds nothing):
pdata.header["early_stop"] = True

title: Examples
text:
Worked examples of the two core concepts:

Pipeline data — building, accessing, mutating, and persisting xData containers.
Pipelines — composing, inspecting, and evaluating xPipeline objects.

title: Installation
text: xileh targets Python 3.11+.

title: Installation
text:
From PyPI
pip install xileh

title: Installation
text:
From source
git clone git@github.com:bsdlab/xileh.git
cd xileh
pip install .
# or, for an editable install during development:
pip install -e .

title: Installation
text:
Development environment
The project uses uv for dependency management. To set up a development environment with all optional groups:
uv sync --extra all
Optional dependency groups:

dev — pytest, pytest-cov, ruff, ipython
docs — Quarto / quartodoc documentation tooling
build — build, twine for packaging
all — everything above

title: xileh
text:
xileh is a lightweight, composable pipeline abstraction built around a hierarchical data container. It generalizes the sklearn.Pipeline idea beyond a feature matrix X and target y to arbitrary, nested data structures.
The library is built from two components:

title: xileh
text:
Design philosophy
xileh deliberately aims to:

impose as few restrictions on your workflow as possible (arbitrary data objects and plain Python functions),
integrate easily with a function-based workflow during development,
provide a single source of truth for the processing,
enable reuse of whole pipelines by strongly motivating composition.

Take sklearn.Pipeline as the motivating example: it is great for building, storing, and evaluating ML pipelines, but it is specific about the shape of data it processes and how (fit / transform). xileh trades built-in functionality for maximal freedom: a single object describing the full pipeline, over any data.

title: xileh
text:
Where to next?

Installation
Quick Start
Examples
API Reference

title: Contributing
text:
git clone git@github.com:bsdlab/xileh.git
cd xileh
uv sync --extra all

title: Contributing
text:
git clone git@github.com:bsdlab/xileh.git
cd xileh
uv sync --extra all

title: Contributing
text:
Branching model

main — stable, released code. Documentation and PyPI publishing trigger off this branch.
develop — integration branch for ongoing work.
Feature branches — feature/<name>, fix/<name>, etc., branched off develop (or main for hotfixes), merged back via pull request.

title: Contributing
text:
Commit messages
Follow Conventional Commits:

feat: a new feature
fix: a bug fix
docs: documentation only
test: adding or fixing tests
refactor: code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
chore: tooling / packaging changes

title: Contributing
text:
Running checks
# tests with coverage
uv run --extra dev pytest tests/ -v --cov=src/xileh

# lint
uv run --extra dev ruff check src/ tests/

title: Contributing
text:
Code style

NumPy-style docstrings for public functions and classes.
Type hints on function signatures.
Keep changes surgical; prefer established libraries over custom code.

title: Contributing
text:
Releasing

Bump version in pyproject.toml.
Tag the commit: git tag vX.Y.Z && git push origin vX.Y.Z.
The publish-pypi workflow builds and publishes to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing.

title: Quick Start
text: This walks through the two core ideas: building an xData container and running an xPipeline over it.

title: Quick Start
text:
A container

from xileh import xData

pdata = xData(
    [
        xData([1, 2, 3, 4], name="array_1"),
        xData("string", name="string_1"),
    ],
    name="outer_container",
)
pdata

xData object at 0x10da0f810 - with size 24
outer_container:    
|   array_1:    list
|   string_1:   <class 'str'>
'--------------------


Containers are accessed by name in three equivalent ways:

assert pdata.get_by_name("array_1") is pdata["array_1"] is pdata.array_1
pdata["array_1"].data

[1, 2, 3, 4]

title: Quick Start
text:
A pipeline
A pipeline step is any function that takes an xData and returns it.

from xileh import xPipeline


def add_ones(pdata, name="ones", size=3):
    pdata.add([1] * size, name=name)
    return pdata


pl = xPipeline("demo", silent=True)
pl.add_steps(
    ("add_ones", add_ones),
    ("add_more", add_ones, {"name": "more_ones", "size": 5}),
)

root = xData([], name="root")
pl.eval(root)
root

xData object at 0x11011ca50 - with size 24
root:   
|   ones:   list
|   more_ones:  list
'--------------------


xPData remains available as a backwards-compatible alias for xData.
Continue with the pipeline data and pipelines examples for the full API.

title: Pipeline data
text: The xData container has three elements:

title: Pipeline data
text:
Initializing a container

from xileh import xData

# single data container -> nothing can be added
pdata = xData("somedata", name="mycontainer")

# a container that can be dynamically extended (data is a list)
pdata = xData([], name="mycontainer")

# with header data
pdata = xData([], name="cont1", header={"description": "some useful container"})

# nested initialization
pdata = xData(
    [xData([1, 2, 3, 4], name="array_1")],
    name="outer_container_1",
)
pdata

xData object at 0x10f354610 - with size 24
outer_container_1:  
|   array_1:    list
'--------------------

title: Pipeline data
text:
Get and set

import numpy as np

# the three accessors are equivalent
assert (
    pdata.get_by_name("array_1")
    is pdata["array_1"]
    is pdata.array_1
)

# setting the .data attribute
pdata["array_1"].data = np.ones(3)
pdata.array_1.data

array([1., 1., 1.])


Whole containers can be overwritten via the dict or attribute accessor:

pdata["array_1"] = xData(np.zeros(3), name="array_1")
pdata.array_1.data

array([0., 0., 0.])

title: Pipeline data
text:
Add and delete

# explicit, via get_by_name
newc = pdata.get_by_name("newcont", create_if_missing=True)
newc.data = np.ones(5)

# or more concise
pdata.add(np.ones(3), name="another", header={"description": "a few ones"})

pdata.delete_by_name("newcont")
pdata.get_container_names()

['outer_container_1', 'array_1', 'another']

title: Pipeline data
Pipeline data
text:
Save and load

import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from xileh.core.pipelinedata import from_container

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    fname = Path(tmp, "test_container")
    pdata.save(fname)
    reloaded = from_container(fname)

reloaded

xData object at 0x107eef8d0 - with size 24
outer_container_1:  
|   array_1:    <class 'numpy.ndarray'>
|   another:    <class 'numpy.ndarray'>
'--------------------

tqdm
utils.progress.tqdm(*args, show_bar: bool = True, **kwargs)
tqdm wrapper with a show_bar kwarg and DISABLE_PROGRESS_BAR env var support.
The bar is suppressed when either: - show_bar=False is passed, or - the environment variable DISABLE_PROGRESS_BAR is set to any non-empty value.
